_note: Harm and misuse vector analysis: researcher's own affordance reasoning. Keyed by platform name. Never auto-generated.

affordances: Peer-to-peer network linking users' computers for file sharing
User's computer acts as mini-server making files available to other users
Download / receive files from the network
Exchange or trade files with other users on the network
Search the network by keyword or filename
Make files available for download from a user's IP address
misuse_surface: Download and save CSAM collections from a peer-to-peer file-sharing network
Exchange or trade CSAM video and image files with other users on the network
Make CSAM files available for other users to download from the offender's computer
Search the network for CSAM using known search terms
Participate in peer-to-peer networks under law-enforcement investigation of CSAM sharing activity
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed, traded, or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child) (ncmec_2024_891; idaho_icac_2024_022; doj_ceos_2026_002)

affordances: Persistent server/community structure (topic-based, invite-linked)
Public and private channels within servers
Real-time text, voice, and video
Direct messaging alongside group spaces
Screen-sharing and livestreaming (Go Live)
File and image sharing in channels and DMs
Pseudonymous accounts, low-friction creation
Bot and webhook integration
Invite-link distribution (servers spread by shareable link)
misuse_surface: Closed/private servers as low-visibility spaces to share material
Server invite-links used to gather offenders or victims into a controlled space
Real-time livestream/screen-share to direct abuse or production as it happens
Community structure used to organize and coordinate among multiple offenders
DM-to-server migration; pull a contact from public channel into private space
Pseudonymity layered with server gating to limit outside visibility
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed, traded, or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is contacted and exploited through server channels and direct messaging
A child is sextorted (coerced under threat after imagery is obtained)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Cloud storage and file hosting without Google account dependency
Shared folder links with public or restricted access controls
No content-type restriction on upload (images, video, archives)
Persistent file availability without sender action
Desktop sync client — automatic local-to-cloud mirroring without manual upload
Third-party app integration
Link-based sharing without recipient account requirement
misuse_surface: Shared links used to distribute CSAM collections without direct file transfer
Desktop sync client used to automatically mirror local CSAM library to cloud
Folder-level sharing to hand off bulk material in a single link
No-account-required link access lowers barrier to receiving material
Used as handoff layer — link shared via separate contact channel (forum, messaging app)
Persistent hosting until platform detection or account termination
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is possessed or stored in an offender's cloud storage account
A child's abuse imagery is uploaded to cloud file hosting for distribution or collection
A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared via cloud storage links (re-victimizing the depicted child)

affordances: Real-name-oriented profiles with rich personal detail
Friend network and mutual-connection discovery
Profile creation with minimal verification (fake accounts trivial)
Public and private messaging (Messenger)
Groups, pages, and event surfaces
Photo/video sharing and tagging
Search by name, school, location, interests
Friend suggestions / "People You May Know" graph traversal
misuse_surface: Fake/impersonation accounts to approach minors under false identity
Mining public profile detail (school, age, location) to target and tailor approach
Friend-network traversal to locate a victim's peers and siblings
Messenger as private channel to move contact out of public view
Building false rapport using profile-derived personal information
Migration target; initiate on Facebook, move to private/ephemeral platform
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is contacted and exploited through profiles and Messenger
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Text-to-image / image-to-image generation
Image-to-image manipulation of existing photos (face-swap, de-aging, nudification)
Conversational chatbots (persona simulation, sustained interaction)
Voice synthesis and cloning
Low/no skill barrier to produce realistic synthetic media
Rapid generation at scale (many outputs, fast)
Open-source / locally-runnable models (no platform oversight)
Fine-tuning on a target's images (likeness capture)
misuse_surface: Generating synthetic CSAM from text prompts (no real victim in the image) (ncmec_2024_1251: prompt-to-image program)
Manipulating a real child's benign photo into abuse imagery (nudification of an actual minor) (mt_doj_2025_001)
Chatbots used to generate CSAM from photos of children the offender knows (doj_ceos_2025_002)
Fine-tuning or prompt engineering to produce targeted synthetic abuse at scale (idaho_icac_2024_026: thousands of AI-generated images)
Chatbots used to simulate a minor, or to automate grooming conversations at scale
Voice cloning for impersonation in contact/extortion
Local model use to evade platform-side detection entirely
harm_vector: A real child's likeness or benign photo is manipulated or synthesized into abuse imagery without requiring contact during creation (mt_doj_2025_001; lapd_2025_002; ncmec_2024_164)
Synthetic CSAM is created via text prompts or chatbots without a separate traditional platform contact phase (doj_ceos_2025_002; ncmec_2024_1251; ncmec_2024_1070)
AI-generated or AI-labeled CSAM is possessed or distributed at scale under new statutory charge types (idaho_icac_2026_001; arkansas_dps_2025_005; scag_icac_2025_019)
Stated evidence N=14 (inferred N=9) in Q1 corpus — harm is production/possession-first, not contact-first (contrast TikTok/Instagram)

affordances: Cloud storage and file hosting at scale (any file type, any size within quota)
Shareable links with configurable access controls (anyone-with-link or specific accounts)
No content-type restriction on upload (documents, images, video, archives)
Persistent availability — files remain accessible indefinitely without sender action
Folder-level organization and bulk sharing (entire directory via single link)
Google account integration with low-friction creation
Download enablement (recipients can pull local copies of shared content)
misuse_surface: Shared links used to distribute CSAM to one or many recipients without direct file transfer
Folder-level sharing used to hand off an entire collection in a single link
Persistent hosting — material stays accessible indefinitely unless platform detects and removes it
Anyone-with-link access removes need for recipient account — lowers barrier to receiving material
Used as handoff layer: offender hosts on Drive, shares link via separate contact channel
Lower detection surface than direct file transfer through messaging
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is possessed or stored in an offender's cloud storage account
A child's abuse imagery is uploaded to cloud file hosting for distribution or collection
A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared via cloud storage (re-victimizing the depicted child)

affordances: Visual-first profiles (photo/video centric)
Public-by-default accounts and discoverability
DM and disappearing-message options
Follower/following graph and suggested accounts
Hashtag and explore-page content discovery
Story format (ephemeral, view-tracked)
Low-friction account creation, multiple accounts per user
Identity/handle takeover potential (account compromise)
misuse_surface: Public visual profiles used to identify and select minor targets
DM as direct private contact channel to a minor
Disappearing messages to reduce forensic trail during solicitation
Explore/hashtag discovery to find minors by interest or age signal
Account compromise/identity takeover to contact a victim's network under a trusted handle (ncmec_2023_430)
Migration target; move contact to ephemeral or encrypted platform
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through DMs and visual profiles
A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is sextorted (coerced under threat after imagery is obtained)

affordances: Username-only registration (no phone number required)
Anonymity by design (no real identity tied to account)
Pseudonymous handles, easily created and discarded
Public group/chat discovery by interest or keyword
Bot and third-party integration within chats
Image and video sharing in direct and group messages
Perceived private or encrypted messaging
misuse_surface: Anonymous contact with minors without traceable identity
Account churn; discard and recreate handles to evade blocks and detection
Interest-based group discovery used to locate victims or trade material
CSAM trading in direct messages and closed groups with low visibility
Offenders cite encryption/privacy as operational cover (anchorage_pd_2024_002 calls it "an encrypted chat platform")
Migration target; offenders move victims onto Kik for the anonymity layer
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed, traded, or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is contacted and exploited through anonymous messaging
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: Random stranger pairing (no mutual connection, no account required)
Anonymous text and video chat (no persistent identity)
No account creation required — zero onboarding friction
Interest tag matching (common tags used to filter pairings)
Unmonitored by design — no moderation infrastructure at scale
No logging or message persistence by default
misuse_surface: Random pairing used to reach minors at scale without targeting effort
Zero-identity architecture — no traceability after session ends
Interest tags used to filter for younger users or shared interests with minors
Unmonitored video channel used to solicit or produce real-time imagery
Session ephemerality — no record of contact, no forensic trail
Migration target — initiate on Omegle, move victim to persistent messaging platform
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through anonymous random video or text chat
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting
A child is solicited for explicit imagery or subjected to real-time sexual exhibition in video chat

affordances: Ephemeral messaging (timer-based auto-deletion)
Disappearing media (view-once photo/video)
Quick-add / username & QR contact discovery
Real-time photo/video capture-and-send (camera-first)
Persistent contact via Snap Map / location
Low-friction account creation
misuse_surface: Evidence destruction by design: content gone before it can be preserved
Solicitation and CSAM transmission with reduced forensic trail
Stranger-to-minor contact via username sharing / quick-add
Real-time coercion of imagery capture using camera-first send
Migration target: offenders move victims to Snapchat for the ephemerality
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through ephemeral messaging
A child's abuse imagery is distributed or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting

affordances: End-to-end encryption
Large-file transfer
Channel broadcasting
Self-destruct timers
misuse_surface: Encrypted channels distribute CSAM collections outside platform detection range
Large-file transfer delivers bulk material directly to named recipients
Broadcast channels reach subscriber audiences of thousands without individual offender-to-recipient contact
Self-destruct timers reduce the forensic trail from direct exchanges
Minimal phone-number-only registration enables pseudonymous operation and rapid account replacement after action
harm_vector: A child's abuse imagery is distributed, traded, or re-shared (re-victimizing the depicted child) (ncmec_2022_546; ncmec_2022_292; ncmec_2023_338; ncmec_2024_214; ncmec_2024_991; ncmec_2025_333; ncmec_2025_437; ncmec_2025_598; ncmec_2025_607; ncmec_2025_672; doj_ceos_2025_014; ncmec_2025_208; wy_dci_2026_001)
A child is contacted and exploited through Telegram messaging (svicac_2022_001)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting (svicac_2022_001)

affordances: Algorithmic For You feed (content surfaced by engagement, not follows)
Short-form video creation and discovery
Public-by-default accounts, broad discoverability
Direct messaging
Duet / stitch / comment interaction with strangers
Live streaming with real-time gifting
Low-friction account creation, large minor user base
Profile and content visible without mutual connection
misuse_surface: Algorithmic discovery used to surface and reach minors at scale
Public videos used to identify and select targets by age signal
DM as private channel to move contact off the public feed
Comment/duet interaction to initiate contact under low suspicion
Migration target; initiate on TikTok, move to private/ephemeral platform (pa_ag_2026_004: "met on TikTok... continued to communicate")
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through TikTok messaging (pa_ag_2026_004; south_florida_icac_2025_005; tbi_icac_2023_003; south_florida_icac_2024_002)
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting (pa_ag_2026_004)
A child is solicited for explicit imagery through TikTok messages (south_florida_icac_2025_005; tbi_icac_2023_003)

affordances: Anonymous posting by default (no account required to browse)
Location-based content discovery (posts surfaced by geographic proximity)
Image-based posts with text overlay (visual-first, low-identity format)
Direct messaging between users with no prior connection required
Interest/topic-based discovery (posts tagged and surfaced by category)
No verified identity or phone number required
Ephemeral community feel — posts decay in visibility over time
misuse_surface: Anonymous contact with minors without traceable identity
Location-based discovery used to identify and approach nearby minors
DM used to move contact off the anonymous public feed into private channel
Interest tags used to identify and surface vulnerable or younger users
Low-identity architecture makes account accountability near-zero
Migration target — initiate on Whisper, move victim to messaging platform
harm_vector: A child is contacted and exploited through anonymous messaging
A child is groomed or enticed toward sexual contact or meeting
